2606.06738.title: Modular Monolingual Adaptation using Pretrained Language Models
2606.06738.paper_summary: Building monolingual language models (LMs) for low-resource languages typically relies on adapting pretrained language models (PLMs) by finetuning the whole model on the target language. This approach is widely favored over training from scratch, as it enables effective knowledge transfer. Additionally, prior work has shown that using a language-specific tokenizer can enhance the adaptability. In this work, we hypothesize that full model tuning is often unnecessary and propose a more modular approach. Specifically, we replace the tokens, freeze the corresponding embeddings, and tune the rest of the model. We use Scottish Gaelic, Irish, and Quechua for our experiments, with Quechua being a very low-resource language (8.5k training instances). Evaluation on natural language understanding (NLU) tasks -- mask filling, NER, and POS -- shows that our proposed approach improves performance when adapting models to low-resource languages. Additionally, we provide a comprehensive analysis of the effectiveness of training strategies, the choice of pretrained embeddings, and models.
2606.06738.paper_summary_zh: 建立單語言模型（LM）以支援資源匱乏語言通常依賴於通過在目標語言上微調整個模型來調整預訓練語言模型（PLM）。<lb/>這種方法被廣泛青睞於從零開始訓練，因為它能夠有效地轉移知識。<lb/>此外，先前的研究顯示，使用特定語言的標記器可以增強適應性。<lb/>在這項工作中，我們假設完全模型調整通常是不必要的，並提出一種更模組化的方法。<lb/>具體而言，我們替換標記，凍結相應的嵌入，並調整模型的其餘部分。<lb/>我們使用蘇格蘭蓋爾語、愛爾蘭語和克丘亞語進行實驗，其中克丘亞語是一種資源非常匱乏的語言（8.5k訓練實例）。<lb/>在自然語言理解（NLU）任務——掩碼填充、命名實體識別（NER）和詞性標註（POS）上進行的評估顯示，我們提出的方法在將模型適應於資源匱乏語言時提高了性能。<lb/>此外，我們提供了對訓練策略、預訓練嵌入的選擇和模型有效性的全面分析。
2606.06738.author: Nalin Kumar et.al.
